MetaSkill / product architecture

Run & Review

proposed

A local, artifact-first evaluation experience: run one task, inspect what the skill made, leave notes, fix it with the agent, then graduate to a small judged batch.

On this page

Product thesis Two gears Viewer Artifacts Judge learning Architecture Build order

Product thesis

Codex runs the skill. The IAB lets the user inspect exactly what it made. The user leaves notes, discusses them with the agent, and only introduces formal cases and a judge after the skill is already useful.

run one task
→
inspect artifact
→
leave notes
→
agent fixes skill
→
run again

When the skill is good enough, the same review experience expands from one output to a batch of 3–8 outputs with automated judgment.

Two gears, one viewer

Manual iteration

One prompt, one skill version, the response and produced files. The user inspects the result and writes a note; the agent reads it and revises the skill.

default for early skill development

Small-batch evaluation

Three to eight saved cases run together. A judge reviews each output using the current rubric; the user inspects concerns and annotates exceptions.

confidence before recurring or automated use

There is no benchmark mode and no lifecycle dashboard. A single run opens directly into focused review; a batch opens into a compact matrix that drills into the same focused review.

The end-user viewer

Focused result

Run 4 · “Create a bakery landing page”Completed
Output files
● index.html
styles.css
app.js
hero.png

Open folder · Open original

Preview
Rendered website or Codex artifact preview

Full IAB tab · viewport controls · source

Your notes
Mobile navigation wraps into the hero. The rest looks strong.

Saved against index.html

The priority is artifact → notes → previous result → response → transcript. Previous output and previous feedback remain available without dominating the screen.

Batch matrix

CaseResultJudgeHuman
Quarterly workbookPassLooks goodReviewed
Landing pageConcernMobile navigationNeeds review
Investor presentationPassLooks good—
Product requirementsRunning——

The matrix is a queue, not the review surface. Selecting a row opens the focused result above. Stable URLs preserve run, case, candidate and artifact.

Artifact behavior

OutputDefault treatmentAlways available
XLSX, DOCX, PPTX, PDFOpen through Codex Artifact PreviewOriginal file and containing folder
Images, text, Markdown, JSONCheap inline previewOpen original
HTML website directoryIsolated live website previewFull IAB tab, source and folder
Unknown binaryMetadata onlyOpen original

Generated HTML is a runnable artifact. Serve the frozen output directory from an isolated local origin or sandboxed iframe so relative CSS, JavaScript and images work. Never inject generated HTML into the workbench DOM.

Annotations teach the judge explicitly

human annotations
→
compare with judge
→
propose rubric change
→
approve or edit
→
future automation

The judge does not silently rewrite itself. Annotations become calibration examples. After a batch, a calibration pass identifies disagreements and proposes reusable rubric changes, each linked to the supporting outputs.

Judge calibrationRubric v2 → v3

Proposed addition. Presentation artifacts should include visible source attribution when supplied material contains external claims.

Supported by: Investor presentation · Market analysis deck

Decisions: Accept · Edit · Keep case-specific

Approved rubric versions are evaluated against prior labeled examples, including a small holdout, before they become the default for automated runs.

Minimum local control plane

ComponentResponsibilityNot responsible for
Run storeImmutable local tasks, outputs, artifacts and provenanceEditing skill source
Artifact gatewayBrowse folders, open originals, preview supported files and safely serve sitesRebuilding Office applications
Review viewerLive progress, focused review, batch matrix and annotationsGenerating skill improvements
JudgeReview outputs with a frozen rubric and cite evidenceSilently changing the rubric
CalibratorCompare judge decisions with human annotations and propose rubric revisionsAutomatically accepting universal rules

The filesystem remains authoritative. The IAB projects live and historical state from .skill/runs/; Codex remains responsible for discussing notes and editing the skill.

Build order

  1. 1. Complete the manual loop

    One-run focused viewer, output-folder navigation, Codex preview handoff, safe live HTML, artifact-targeted notes, previous output and stable URLs.

  2. 2. Add the 3–8-case batch

    Live matrix, focused drill-down, judge review, needs-review filtering and selective reruns.

  3. 3. Calibrate the judge

    Human–judge disagreement review, proposed rubric amendments, explicit approval, versioning and alignment checks.

Build the focused artifact-review loop first. It is the daily product and the foundation for every later case, judge and calibration workflow.

Borrow Anthropic’s immediate output-to-feedback handoff and Tessl’s durable run identity and live batch progress. Differentiate with native Codex artifact inspection, runnable HTML websites and transparent judge calibration.

Reference comparison

Anthropic: one output at a time, files, formal grades, one feedback string and previous iteration context. HTML is shown as source; feedback does not automatically tune the grader.

Tessl: strong durable run URLs, progress, history and aggregate-to-scenario drill-down. Public documentation does not describe ad hoc artifact review, annotations or judge learning.

Sources: Anthropic Skill Creator · Anthropic eval viewer · Tessl scenario evaluations · LangSmith judge alignment
